topic: Cherry juice indicator scale and beakers

render_data.title: Cherry juice indicator — match each beaker to its colour
render_data.prompt: Cherry juice changes colour with pH (Red pH 1–3.5, Orange pH 3.5–6, Purple pH 6–9, Green pH 9–12). Place each beaker into the colour the cherry juice would turn.

image_prompt: SVG: illustration of four chilli peppers labelled Habanero pepper (bulbous orange), Jalapeño pepper (green cluster), Red chilli pepper (slender red), Cayenne pepper (long curved red), plus a speech bubble from a person: 'The red chilli pepper will have the most capsaicin as it tastes spicier than the others.'

SVG: two displayed structures of capsaicin-family molecules. Structure 1 (16 000 000 SHU): 'Remainder of the molecule' box –CH2–N(H)–C(=O)–CH2–CH2–CH2–CH2–CH=CH–CH(CH3)2 with a C=C double bond near the end. Structure 2 (9 100 000 SHU): same amide head but a fully saturated, slightly longer chain –CH2–N(H)–C(=O)–CH2–CH2–CH2–CH2–CH2–CH(CH3)2 with no C=C double bond. Key differences: C=C double bond present in structure 1, and structure 1 has the unsaturation/branched terminus.
